# artifactuploadcli

> A Bun + TypeScript CLI that uploads a file to Cloudflare R2 (printing the public download URL as a terminal QR code), or serves a file over the local network with a QR code — no R2 needed for serving.

Runtime: **Bun** (not node). Source is TypeScript ESM run directly; there is no build step.

## Commands

```bash
# Upload to R2, print URL + QR code
bun src/index.ts <file> [options]

# Serve a file over the LAN until Ctrl+C (no R2 config needed)
bun src/index.ts serve <file> [options]

# If installed globally (bin name)
artifactuploadcli <file> [options]
artifactuploadcli serve <file> [options]
```

## Upload options

- `-b, --bucket <name>` — R2 bucket (env: `R2_BUCKET`)
- `-u, --public-url <url>` — public URL prefix (env: `R2_PUBLIC_URL`)
- `-n, --name <name>` — remote object name (default: `{name}-{timestamp}{ext}`)
- `--no-qr` — skip QR code
- `-h, --help` — show help

## Serve options

- `-p, --port <n>` — port to bind (default: 8787, auto-falls back to next free port up to +20)
- `--no-qr` — skip QR code

## Configuration (upload only)

Set in a `.env` file in the **current working directory** (gitignored), or pass as flags:

```
R2_ACCOUNT_ID=...
R2_ACCESS_KEY_ID=...
R2_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY=...
R2_BUCKET=...
R2_PUBLIC_URL=https://...
```

`serve` needs none of these — it only reads the file and binds a local port.

## Examples

```bash
# Upload an APK and get a scannable download link
artifactuploadcli ./app-release.apk

# Upload with a fixed remote name, no QR
artifactuploadcli ./build.ipa -n latest.ipa --no-qr

# Share a file with someone on the same Wi-Fi
artifactuploadcli serve ./video.mp4 -p 9000
```

## Notes for agents

- Always invoke with `bun` (per project convention); do not use `node` or `npm`.
- Typecheck with `tsc --noEmit` (or `bun run check`). No tests, no emit.
- The S3 client sets checksum calc to `WHEN_REQUIRED` — required for R2 compatibility; do not remove.
- Source files import siblings with `.js` extensions despite being `.ts` (bundler moduleResolution + ESM).
